§6Low-Density Element§f

§aLightweight structural components — essential for Soyuz rockets and orbital modules!§f

§bLow-Density Elements§f (hbm:part_generic:3) are lightweight structural components used in aerospace applications. They are forged in the §bPlasma Forge§f and require §bStellar Flux§f as a key ingredient.

§6Production§f:
Forged in the §bPlasma Forge§f using advanced materials including stellar flux.
Requires §e10 MHE/t§f and §e1 MTU/t§f power input.

§6Uses§f:
§b• §bSoyuz-FG Rocket§f — requires §e32§f low-density elements per rocket
§b• §bOrbital Module§f — requires 1 low-density element
§b• §bGerald The Construction Android§f — used in various assembly recipes

§eTip§f: You'll need a significant number of low-density elements for the Soyuz-FG rocket alone (32 per launch). Each orbital module also needs one. Stockpile these before attempting satellite or Gerald launches!
